mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50 percent in a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production confirms broad-based economic strength that boosts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series benchmark by more than 0.04 Rank IC over a 36-month evaluation period, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of co-occurring expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates capacity utilization approaching limits, prompting equity investors to price in imminent monetary tightening and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of late-cycle expansion months with rising INDPRO, or the mechanism shows no directional edge compared to a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve rate hikes that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 58 percent of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal information coefficient falls below zero across all 24-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports consumer demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year out-of-sample period, or 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months where both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with positive GDP growth during a post-shock recovery phase indicate renewed capital investment and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of triggered recovery-phase observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side momentum that reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months with rising industrial production, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 across all 36-month rolling evaluation windows.
